title: What gets committed vs generated
question: Should the repo track the rendered viewer, or only the decision source?
rationale: Track source only - the decision JSON, _project.json, icon, and the generator. The viewer files (index.html/app.js/styles.css/graph.html/manifest.json) and the local .claude/ config are generated or machine-specific, so they're gitignored and built on clone. This was forced by a real bug: locally the viewer files are absolute symlinks into the shared template (d28), so committing them leaked the home path and left dangling symlinks that broke the example for anyone who cloned. Generating on clone always works and keeps the repo clean. The path was also scrubbed from git history.

options.label: Track source only; generate the viewer on clone

tradeoffs.note: the committed symlinks were absolute paths - broken for anyone who cloned; generating locally always works
no machine paths or username left in the repo or its history
one extra step - run the generator - before the example explorer opens

options.label: Commit the rendered viewer into decisions/

tradeoffs.note: the example opens with no build step
the files are absolute symlinks to the shared viewer; they dangle and leak the home path once cloned
